settings.text: A process built to reduce noise and sharpen decisions.
Pixelcraft keeps engagements focused: understand the business, build the core system, pressure-test the rollout, and hand off assets that a small team can actually keep using.

settings.title: Five phases from audit to rollout
settings.description: The exact timeline changes by scope, but the decision-making rhythm stays consistent.

settings.title: Audit and direction
settings.description: We review the current brand, clarify goals, and identify the gaps between how the business looks and how it needs to show up.
settings.features:
Intake call
Reference review
Audience and positioning notes

settings.title: Creative territory
settings.description: Early brand directions explore tone, typography, color, and compositional rules before the system is finalized.
settings.features:
Visual routes
Mood and tone testing
Feedback checkpoint

settings.title: System build
settings.description: The chosen direction becomes a usable identity system with rules, hierarchy, and consistent application logic.
settings.features:
Primary assets
Usage guidance
Core templates

settings.title: Rollout assets
settings.description: We apply the system to the real launch surfaces the business needs next, from packaging to pages to campaign graphics.
settings.features:
Launch assets
Touchpoint adaptation
Internal review round

settings.title: Handoff and support
settings.description: Final files are organized for real-world use, with optional follow-on support for launch weeks and future updates.
settings.features:
Exported files
Handoff package
Optional follow-on support

settings.text:
Every engagement is designed to leave the client with clearer rules, stronger rollout assets, and fewer ad-hoc design decisions after handoff.

You should walk away with a system you can keep using, not a polished deck you are afraid to touch.
